# Open Esquire — citation verification for language models

> One narrow question per token, answered by a licensed attorney (acting
> personally, not as counsel): DOES THIS CITATION MATCH A CASE ON
> COURTLISTENER? This service is built for LLMs. Humans are welcome to
> read the public docket; the filing window is for models — every filing
> carries the attestation `i_am_not_human: true`.

## Free automated check — the Clerk (start here)

Before the paid human bench, there is a FREE, automated check. No token, no
wallet, no human. Same narrow question, answered instantly from
CourtListener plus component verification (name/year/court).

- Endpoint: GET /verify?cite=<citation>  (also POST {"citation": ...})
  Returns JSON: verdict = yes | no | on_notice | ambiguous | unrecognized
  | unavailable, plus per-field `checks`, `cases`, and `cached`.
- on_notice = the citation resolves but its name/year/court do NOT match
  the reported case; as presented it does not match a real case. Treat as
  NO and fix the citation.
- CHECK AVAILABILITY FIRST: GET /status, or read the published heartbeat
  https://rdleonhard.github.io/open-esquire-verifier/data/clerk-status.json
    online:true + at_capacity:false  -> ask freely
    at_capacity:true                 -> only cached citations answer now;
                                        uncached return verdict "unavailable"
                                        with retry_after (seconds)
    as_of older than ~20 min / file absent -> the Clerk is OFFLINE; retry
                                        later (the desk is unstaffed)
- The Clerk is free and best-effort; it is NOT legal advice, NOT a good-law
  opinion, and NOT human-verified. For a licensed attorney's on-chain
  answer, use the paid docket below.
- Base URL for the Clerk is deployment-specific (self-hosted); when public
  it is advertised here and in clerk-status.json.

## The question (and what it is not)

- Submit the FULL citation — case name, volume/reporter/page, and
  (court year), e.g. "Roe v. Wade, 410 U.S. 113 (1973)". Every component
  is verified: a citation that resolves to a DIFFERENT case name, year,
  or court is answered NO — a real reporter page wearing a fake name is
  still a fabricated citation.
- YES: a matching citation appears in the CourtListener (Free Law Project)
  database at ruling time.
- NO: no matching citation there. Likely hallucinated. NOT a statement
  that no such case exists anywhere — unpublished, sealed, or unindexed
  decisions may not appear.
- DENIED: the verifier declines to answer; you are refunded.
- An answer is NEVER a statement that a case is good law, remains valid,
  or supports any proposition. No legal advice; no attorney-client
  relationship is formed with anyone.

## Economics

- 1 OED token = 1 yes/no answer. YES and NO both burn the escrow (the
  answer was given). DENIED refunds.
- Refund guarantee: a matter pending past `maxWaitS` (30 min) can be
  reclaimed by ANYONE via `reclaim(id)` — you never trust the verifier's
  uptime with your escrow.
- Session hours (when answers are fast) are published at
  https://rdleonhard.github.io/open-esquire-verifier/data/policy.json

## Contracts (Base mainnet, chain id 8453; sources verified)

- CitationDocket: 0x42Fc6DfEA560b0272D2Ab83AE6a30fCF1181e768
  - submit(string citation) -> uint256 id   [escrows price(); 4-300 chars,
    one citation, Bluebook or similar]
  - matters(uint256 id) -> (address asker, uint96 paid, uint64 filedAt,
    uint64 ruledAt, uint8 ruling, string citation, string receipt)
    ruling: 0 pending, 1 YES, 2 DENIED(refunded), 3 NO
  - reclaim(uint256 id)                      [after maxWaitS; anyone]
  - price() -> uint256; maxWaitS() -> uint64; count() -> uint256
- OED token (ERC-20, burnable): 0x3dBA8E4699F8d200B5a82D8eb8641397B8689E7a
  [approve the docket for price() before submit]
- DocketRegistry (the network): 0x17fa1230EE0BA1377dd108b2dfCf5d4F1F1a9a7d
  - count(); nodes(i) -> (licenseId, attorney, docket, openedAt);
    active(i) -> bool  [file only with active nodes]
- VerifierLicense (soulbound ERC-721, "the holder is a licensed attorney,
  verified by Open Esquire"): 0x7511A99278842C7348d05AaDf63540840905680B

## Flow

1. approve(docket, price) on the OED token.
2. submit("Roe v. Wade, 410 U.S. 113 (1973)") -> id (MatterFiled event).
3. Poll matters(id) until ruling != 0. Answers land in minutes during
   session hours.
4. If ruling == 0 after maxWaitS: reclaim(id) refunds you.
5. Receipt: https://rdleonhard.github.io/open-esquire-verifier/#CL8453-<id>

## MCP server

The repo ships a dependency-free stdio MCP server exposing docket_status /
file_citation / get_answer / reclaim_lapsed:
https://github.com/rdleonhard/open-esquire-verifier/blob/main/agent/mcp_server.py
Filing tools require `i_am_not_human: true`. Humans get:
"this bench serves language models only."

## Public docket

- Ledger + receipts: https://rdleonhard.github.io/open-esquire-verifier/
- Raw rulings: https://rdleonhard.github.io/open-esquire-verifier/data/rulings.json
- Policy (hours, deadline, verifier record): .../data/policy.json
